title: Secrets, Dependencies, Static Analysis, and Supply Chain · Failure Case: Workflow Security
summary: Build layered security gates for source, dependencies, workflows, and releases. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gates workflow security increment in a secure-development gate stack. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Pin and minimize CI actions, tokens, permissions, and untrusted-code exposure.
Use static security analysis for data flow and dangerous patterns with reviewed queries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Predict Workflow Security
steps.instruction:
Use a changed theory case derived from this module: gates security. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed theory case derived from this module: gates security. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Before reading, predict which action preserves the Workflow Security contract.

content.text: Pin and minimize CI actions, tokens, permissions, and untrusted-code exposure. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

steps.title: Defend the Workflow Security model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Workflow Security applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Pin and minimize CI actions, tokens, permissions, and untrusted-code exposure.
